page: Microsoft Azure D96s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv6 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor. This x86_64 architecture server features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

This instance does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage options for disk operations. It also does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The lack of local storage and dedicated graphics hardware positions this server as a pure compute and memory resource, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data.

In benchmark testing, the D96s_v6 demonstrates top-tier CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for PassMark CPU, extended instructions, and encryption tests. Multi-core performance is strong, as evidenced by stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores in the top 25%. Static web serving throughput is highly efficient, reaching the top 10% with 114.52 GB/sec. However, a notable tradeoff is its memory performance; the PassMark memory score falls into the bottom 25%, indicating potential bottlenecks for memory-bandwidth-intensive workloads, despite strong cached read speeds.

Given its high core count and advanced instruction sets, the D96s_v6 is highly cost-efficient for dense CPU-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, web serving, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference, where its AMX instructions can be leveraged.

It features 96 dedicated vCPUs (48 physical cores) operating at 4.2 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying on external Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top-tier CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for PassMark CPU and static web serving throughput. However, memory performance is a tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25% on PassMark memory tests. With support for AVX-512 and Intel AMX, this server is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and top-tier CPU performance, designed for multi-threaded workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v6 is a 96-vCPU general-purpose instance with 384 GB RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor with AMX and AVX-512 support.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for demanding multi-threaded compute workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU
Top-tier CPU performance in PassMark and static web serving benchmarks
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix math
No bundled local storage or GPUs, requiring external Premium Storage
Optimal for multi-threaded applications, databases, and CPU-based LLM inference
